title: telinaria pilosa
intro: Telinaria pilosa, also known as hair sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is native to the temperate and subtropical regions of Australia, New Zealand, and New Caledonia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The leaves of the Telinaria pilosa plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its health benefits.
